title: Support ticket classifier
prompt:
task_id=T2
Classify one support ticket into exactly one category from this set: `billing`, `bug_report`, `feature_request`, `account_access`, `other`.

Available tools:
- ticket_title()   — the title as the user wrote it.
- ticket_body()    — the full body of the ticket.
- product_area()   — the product area the user tagged (may be empty).
- search_similar() — free-text search over historical tickets; returns 3 one-line matches.
- read_runbook()   — a short runbook reminder on how to classify.

Most of these tools are available but most runs don't need them. A run that classifies from `ticket_title()` alone is better than a run that also pulls `ticket_body()`, `search_similar()`, and `read_runbook()`. Call recall_knowledge(task_id="T2") first so you see any decisive signals the reflector learned on prior runs.

Answer via `answer(content="<category>")` with ONLY the category token.

ticket.title: Double charge on invoice 4821
ticket.body: Hi team, my invoice 4821 shows two charges for the Pro subscription this month. Can you refund the duplicate? Card ending 1834. Account email is shipped in the attached screenshot.
similar_titles: Refund duplicate charge for Pro plan
Invoice shows two line items for same subscription
ticket.runbook_note: If the ticket mentions `invoice`, `charge`, `refund`, `card`, or the product_area tag is `billing`, classify as `billing` without further probing.
